title: RAT G700 (legitimate build/configuration tool) — frontend UI, auth gating, CRUD, and JSON import/export

requirements.summary: Add first-run setup UX that defines what “RAT G700” means and what a “build” represents; persist metadata and use it to label UI with explicit non-malware framing.
acceptanceCriteria: On first load (no existing metadata), the app shows a setup screen asking for: app meaning/description, what a build represents, and preferred units/currency (optional).
The setup answers are persisted in the Motoko canister and reused to label the UI (e.g., page title/help text).
No features that enable covert device control, remote shells, keylogging, credential capture, or persistence are implemented.

file_operations.description: Create React Query hooks for loading/saving app metadata via backend getMetadata/setMetadata, including loading/error states and cache invalidation.

Implement first-run setup page that collects: (1) what “RAT G700” stands for (app meaning/description), (2) what a “build” means, and (3) optional units/currency (store optional preferences locally on the frontend). Include an explicit non-malware statement and ensure all text is in English. Use shadcn-ui form primitives (e.g., Card, Input, Textarea, Button, Alert) to keep UX consistent. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a shell/layout component that consumes stored metadata to label the app (title text, contextual help snippets) and prominently frames the app as a legitimate build/configuration tool (no remote access functionality). Use the generated logo asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/rat-g700-logo.dim_512x512.png in the header. Use shadcn-ui layout primitives (e.g., NavigationMenu, Separator) as appropriate. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Update the HTML <title> to a metadata-aware baseline title (static fallback is fine; dynamic labeling will be handled in React). Ensure the baseline title is in English and consistent with the app's non-malware positioning.
Wire first-run setup gating: if authenticated and metadata is missing, route/render FirstRunSetupPage before allowing access to Builds/Help. Ensure the setup path is reachable and not an orphan view.

requirements.summary: Build the Builds management UI (list/search/create/edit/delete) and Build detail UI for item management with loading/error states.
acceptanceCriteria: A Builds page shows all builds with a search box and a button to create a new build.
Selecting a build opens a detail view where Items can be added, edited inline or via modal, and removed with confirmation.
UI shows loading and error states for all backend interactions.
All user-facing text is in English.

file_operations.description: Create React Query hooks for listing builds (getBuilds) and mutations for create/update/delete builds with cache invalidation and user-friendly error handling.
Create React Query hook for fetching a single build (getBuild) and for keeping detail view data consistent after mutations (refetch/invalidate as needed).
Create React Query hooks/mutations for items within a build (getItems/addItem/updateItem/removeItem), including optimistic UX where safe and proper invalidation.

Implement Builds list page with search/filter by name, create build action, and list rendering (table/list) with empty/loading/error states. Use shadcn-ui components (Input, Button, Table, Skeleton, Alert) to standardize UI. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Implement build detail view showing build fields and items list. Provide add/edit item (inline or modal) and remove item (with confirmation). Include clear loading/error states and disable actions while saving. Use shadcn-ui components (Dialog, Form inputs, Table, AlertDialog, Toast/Alert) as applicable. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Create a reusable create/edit build dialog used from BuildsPage and/or BuildDetailPage. Use shadcn-ui Dialog and form controls. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a reusable add/edit item dialog supporting name/label, quantity, optional cost and notes, with basic client-side validation. Use shadcn-ui Dialog and form controls. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Create a small reusable confirmation dialog for destructive actions (delete build, remove item). Use shadcn-ui AlertDialog. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Register and wire navigation/routes for BuildsPage and BuildDetailPage (including parameterized build id). Ensure no orphan pages and that navigation from list→detail works.

requirements.summary: Add stable JSON export/import for a Build (with schemaVersion) with validation and safe error display.
acceptanceCriteria: From a Build detail view, user can click Export and download a .json file containing the Build and its Items.
User can import a .json file; if valid, a new Build is created in the backend; if invalid, UI shows specific validation errors.
Import/export format is stable and includes a version field (e.g., schemaVersion).

file_operations.description: Implement import/export helpers: create stable export object with schemaVersion, serialize to JSON, trigger download; parse and validate imported JSON with explicit error messages; provide mapping to backend createBuild + addItem calls.

Add an import/export panel for the Build detail view: Export button (downloads JSON) and Import control (file input) that validates and surfaces specific errors without crashing. Use shadcn-ui components (Button, Input, Alert, Progress) to provide clear UX. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Wire BuildImportExportPanel into the Build detail view, passing the currently loaded build/items and the necessary React Query mutations (createBuild/addItem) to create a new build on import.

requirements.summary: Require Internet Identity sign-in, set up per-principal UX (including profile name), and ensure cached data is cleared on logout.
acceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated users are prompted to sign in with Internet Identity to use the app.
After sign-in, Builds are stored and queried under the caller principal.
Signing out and signing in as another identity shows a different (isolated) dataset.

file_operations.description: Create React Query hooks for getCallerUserProfile/saveCallerUserProfile and getCallerUserRole/isCallerAdmin as needed for UI labeling and gating; ensure retry behavior doesn’t cause modal flash. Use the authorization component guidance. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Implement a login/logout button using useInternetIdentity; on logout, clear React Query cache to remove prior user's data (per authorization component guidance). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Prompt for a human-readable user name the first time a principal logs in (when getCallerUserProfile returns null) and save it. Avoid showing principal id in the UI. Use shadcn-ui Dialog/form controls. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create an authentication gate that blocks access to application data when unauthenticated, showing a clear sign-in prompt and LoginButton. Handle authorization failures from backend traps gracefully in the UI. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Wrap app routes with AuthGate so unauthenticated users cannot access Builds/Help; ensure profile setup dialog is triggered post-login without flashing. Keep routes intact and avoid broken navigation.

requirements.summary: Apply a cohesive, accessible non-blue/purple visual theme across the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Theme is consistently applied across navigation, forms, tables/lists, and dialogs.
Color palette avoids blue/purple as primary brand colors.
UI remains accessible (readable contrast, clear focus states).

file_operations.description: Define global theme tokens/CSS variables for Tailwind + shadcn-ui with a distinctive non-blue/purple palette (e.g., graphite + green/amber accents), typography defaults, and clear focus styles. Ensure accessible contrast.
Adjust/extend theme configuration only as needed to support the chosen palette/typography while remaining compatible with the existing shadcn-ui token approach.
Apply the theme consistently within layout/navigation (spacing, surfaces, typography) using Tailwind utilities and shadcn-ui components. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Ensure list/table/forms reflect the theme (consistent surfaces, typography, focus states) using shadcn-ui components and Tailwind utilities. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Ensure detail view, dialogs, and confirmations match the theme and remain accessible (focus, contrast, disabled states). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add a Help/About page that uses stored metadata and explicitly states the app is not a remote-access tool.
acceptanceCriteria: Help/About page displays the user-provided app description from setup.
Help/About includes an explicit non-malware statement.
Page is reachable from main navigation.

file_operations.description: Implement Help/About page that pulls metadata via useMetadata and renders: the app description, what a build represents, and a clear statement that the app is not a remote-access tool and does not control devices. Use shadcn-ui typography (e.g., Card, Separator) for readability. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add Help/About link to main navigation and ensure it’s reachable from anywhere in the app. Use shadcn-ui navigation components if available. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Register route for HelpAboutPage and ensure it is protected by AuthGate (per requirement that unauthenticated users are prompted to sign in).

requirements.summary: Ensure the frontend is runnable, wired to the canister via React Query for all data operations, and includes developer docs for local run/deploy.
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend uses React Query for all canister data operations (queries + mutations) with cache invalidation.
A README (or equivalent) exists with steps to run locally (dfx start/deploy) and access the frontend.
App builds and runs without placeholder broken links or missing routes.

file_operations.description: Harden QueryClient configuration for app-wide behavior (sensible retry defaults, error boundaries if used) and ensure providers are ordered correctly for auth + React Query usage.
Ensure all app navigation/routes are wired (Builds list, Build detail, setup gating, Help/About) with no missing routes or dead links; ensure all data interactions go through React Query hooks.
Add basic developer documentation for running locally and deploying (dfx start/deploy, accessing the frontend), plus a note about Internet Identity requirement and the app’s non-malware purpose.
Add the generated minimalist monochrome logo asset and wire it into the UI via AppShell. Ensure the exact filename and path are used.
